title: Executive Order 12961—Presidential Advisory Committee on Gulf War Veterans' Illnesses

flags.justification: A twelve-member committee explicitly excluding full-time federal employees, purely advisory in function.
Reports through three named Secretaries; Sec. 2(f) requires an interim report within six months and a final report by a specific date.
Multiple related review areas (research, coordination, treatment, outreach, risk factors) all coherently serve one purpose: reviewing the government's response to Gulf War veterans' illnesses.
A purely advisory body with no compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
Sec. 4(b) sets a clean, self-executing termination: thirty days after submitting its final report.
Sec. 2(d) explicitly excludes legal-liability determinations from the Committee's scope, a deliberate and sensible boundary preventing the Committee from being misused for that purpose.
A purely advisory body with independent, non-federal-employee membership.
A significant but narrow, task-specific, time-limited response to a real public-health concern; the order's own text claims no ongoing or expansive authority.
No second agency's competing mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/12 = 0.0%. Another exemplary time-limited advisory commission, handling a genuinely sensitive public-health topic (Gulf War Syndrome) with appropriate structural care.
